category_title: At-risk students in the Normal Technical stream exhibiting attention-seeking or disengaged behaviors
scenario_title: Frustration and task avoidance during self-directed learning
minimal_context: The student is in the keyboard lab using SONAR software to learn their instrumental part for 'Count On Me' by listening to pre-recorded tracks.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please put on your headphones and use the SONAR software to listen to the pre-recorded tracks. Your task is to discover and learn your instrumental part for 'Count On Me'.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the computer screen and the keyboard, appearing a bit blurred about the task.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying to figure it out. Listen to the recording.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Does not say anything at all, making it hard to know whether he knows or does not know what to do.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: The others are already focused on learning their parts. You need to start practicing.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Remains completely silent and stops doing his work for some time, visibly frustrated because he cannot figure out what to do.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please put on your headphones and use the SONAR software to listen to the pre-recorded tracks. Your task is to discover and learn your instrumental part for 'Count On Me'.

STUDENT: (Looks at the computer screen and the keyboard, appearing a bit blurred about the task.)

TUTOR: Keep trying to figure it out. Listen to the recording.

STUDENT: (Does not say anything at all, making it hard to know whether he knows or does not know what to do.)

TUTOR: The others are already focused on learning their parts. You need to start practicing.

STUDENT: (Remains completely silent and stops doing his work for some time, visibly frustrated because he cannot figure out what to do.)

task.content: Learning individual instrumental parts (drums, guitar, bass, piano) for the song 'Count On Me' by listening to pre-recorded tracks
task.learning_goal: Discovering things for themselves and learning their parts

context.materials: Computers, headphones, SONAR software with pre-recorded instrumental parts

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: Because he could not figure out what to do
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Not doing his work for some time when the others were already focused

behavior_patterns.condition: When practicing on their own and encountering problems
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: They don't say anything at all, so you really don't know whether they know or do not know
